Followed official links from the Provost AI Policies page to classroom guidance, syllabus examples, and UTech AI resources.

artifacts.summary: Official Case Western Reserve University sources were found on case.edu and official subpaths. The final claim set uses central Provost guidance, UTech guidance, Writing Program guidance, and a School of Medicine PDF with its school-specific scope preserved.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: While utilizing AI tools in academic settings, CWRU remains committed to upholding our Academic Integrity Policy. It is the shared responsibility of students, faculty, and administrators to uphold and maintain these standards.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow central-source claim about CWRU commitment to Academic Integrity Policy when AI tools are used in academic settings.
Approved as a teaching-guidance claim from the Provost source; it does not state a single university-wide student permission rule.
Approved as a narrow claim about sample syllabus text and faculty academic freedom; sample texts are not represented as binding university-wide policy.
Approved as privacy/security guidance from official University Technology source.
Approved as a sourced services/enterprise-protection claim from official University Technology page.
Approved with scope to the Writing Program recommendation, not as a blanket university ban on AI detectors.
Approved with explicit School of Medicine medical-student scope preserved from the PDF.

artifacts.title: Case Western Reserve University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for Case Western Reserve University found official AI guidance on the Provost, Writing Program, University Technology, and School of Medicine pages. Claims are limited to the cited source scopes: central academic-integrity and teaching guidance, UTech privacy and supported-service guidance, Writing Program detector guidance, and a School of Medicine medical-student policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The School of Medicine PDF claim is scoped only to medical students enrolled in the University and College Programs at Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine.
The Writing Program AI detector claim is a program recommendation for writing instruction, not a university-wide prohibition.
The official news result was rejected and not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
